title: carex scirpoidea subsp. stenochlaena
intro: Carex scirpoidea subsp. stenochlaena, also known as Rocky Mountain sedge, is a subspecies of perennial sedge plant native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing irritated mucous membranes. Carex scirpoidea subsp. stenochlaena can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
